summary: Clear audience tape captures a scorching 1978 Madison show highlighted by a soaring “Music Never Stopped” and monster Estimated > Eyes jam.
review: The recording is an audience (AUD) capture that, despite occasional loud spots and minor drop‑outs, remains surprisingly clear and well balanced. Musically the night is legendary – Jerry’s solos on “Music Never Stopped”, the Estimated > Eyes sequence and the marathon Playin’/Wheel jam are repeatedly called out as among the best of ’78. The first set opens with a tight “Cold Rain & Snow” and rolls through classic ’78 gems before the second‑set firestorm.

ai_rating.rationale: Over two dozen reviews overwhelmingly praise the performance (average ★4.5) and note only modest recording quirks; the strong consensus yields a high‑confidence rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well balanced for an AUD, with occasional volume spikes, minor cut/drop‑out spots (e.g., after the Playin' jam) and a bit of reverb but still very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set launches with a crisp “Cold Rain & Snow” and builds through strong ’78 staples; the second set erupts into an epic Estimated > Eyes segue into a marathon Playin'/Wheel jam, delivering one of the era’s most cohesive and high‑energy sequences.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia receives universal praise – his solos on “Music Never Stopped”, Estimated > Eyes, and the long jams are described as cosmic, flawless, and among his best of ’78.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboard work is noted; some reviewers love his interplay (e.g., on Loser) while others mention occasional glissando stumbles, but overall his contributions are seen as integral to the magic.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drummers are mentioned mainly for dropping in during the Playin' jam; no major issues reported.
